my_thoughts: 'I wasn't sure if I could say that'—permission question. Teacher gives explicit permission: 'you can always tell me.' Four turns established: therapist-as-authority doesn't mean therapist-as-infallible. Criticism welcomed. This models healthy relationship: feedback flows both ways.
accumulated_insight: T4 (Final): Student continues 'I wasn't sure if I could say that.' Teacher clarifies: 'You can always tell me if something bothers you.' Four-turn arc: unspoken → invited → denied → named → validated. Teaching that criticism of therapist is permitted and valued.
visible_response: Sometimes you criticize yourself, and sometimes you imagine your conscience is out here in me, when it’s been in you all the time.
